title: Monkey Madness I

{{External|rs=Monkey Madness}}
{{Has quick guide}}
{{Infobox Quest
|name = Monkey Madness I
|number = 65
|image = [[File:Monkey Madness I.png|300px]]
|release = [[6 December]] [[2004]]
|update = Monkey Madness
|members = Yes
|series = [[Quests/Series#Gnome|Gnome]], #4

'''Monkey Madness I''' is the fourth quest in the [[gnome quest series]], and one of the most well-known quests in ''RuneScape.'' The quest brings the player to [[Ape Atoll]], an island inhabited by civilised but unfriendly and high-level [[monkey]]s, in order to help [[Narnode Shareen]] track down his missing [[10th Squad]] military unit.

A grandmaster-difficulty sequel to the quest called [[Monkey Madness II]] was released on [[6 May]] [[2016]]. 

==Details==
{{Quest details
|start = Talk to [[King Narnode Shareen]] in the [[Grand Tree]].
|startmap = 2466,3495
|difficulty = Master
|description = The King of the northern Gnomes, Narnode Shareen, is once again in need of your help. He recently decided to send an envoy of his Royal Guard, the 10th squad, to oversee the decommissioning of the Gnome owned ship-building facilities on the eastern coast of [[Karamja]].

It has been quite some time since the 10th squad were dispatched and they have been deemed missing in action. It will be up to you, should you decide to help, to find out what fate befell the 10th squad and if possible, track them down.

If only it were so simple. Sinister forces have begun to spread through the Gnome hierarchy and threaten to unleash an unknown terror upon the world. Far across the land, the fires of vengeance are being stoked once again. Can you unravel the mystery behind the deception? Can you separate the truth from the lies? Can you decide for yourself what is real and what is not?
|length = Long
|requirements = * Completion of the following quests:
** [[The Grand Tree]]
** [[Tree Gnome Village]]
* The ability to defeat a level 195 [[Jungle Demon]]
|items = * A [[gold bar]]
* Five empty inventory slots
* A [[ball of wool]]
* [[Monkey bones]] or [[Monkey corpse|corpse]] from [[Karamja]] (obtainable during quest)
* Five [[banana]]s (obtainable during quest)
|recommended = * {{SCP|Combat|65|link=yes}}
* {{SCP|Prayer|43+}} [[Prayer]] '''highly recommended''', as the player will be attacked by numerous hard-hitting melee enemies in multicombat
* [[Poison]] cures such as [[Antipoison|antipoison]] or [[Antidote++|antidote++]]
* Energy-restoring items such as the [[explorer's ring]] along with [[energy potion|energy]] and [[stamina potion]]s
* [[Prayer potion]]s
* A [[knife]] for free healing, as there are [[pineapple plants]] around Ape Atoll
* High healing [[food]]
* A [[lockpick]]
* [[Weight-reducing clothing]]
* Emergency teleport (a [[ring of life]] works as well)
* [[Ardougne cloak 1]] or [[Ardougne teleport]]s [[File:Ardougne teleport (tablet).png]]
* A [[ring of dueling]] to bank quickly (the Ardougne cloak could be used instead, although it's somewhat further from a bank; the Battlefield of Khazard has a spirit tree, and the Gnome Stronghold has a bank and glider service)
* 200,000 [[coins]] (if skipping the puzzle)
|kills = * [[Jungle Demon]] ''(level 195)''
|ironman = * {{SCP|Smithing|40|link=yes}} {{Boostable|yes}} (to make the gold bar if not dropped by a monster) or {{SCP|Magic|33|link=yes}} {{Boostable|yes}} (to telegrab a gold bar from [[Varrock west bank]] basement) or {{SCP|Thieving|5|link=yes}} to steal from a [[crafting stall]] in Keldagrim. A gold bar is also a reward from [[The Digsite]], [[Goblin Diplomacy]], and [[Waterfall Quest]]
|leagueRegion = <poem>
Unlocking {{LeagueRegion|Kandarin}} {{okay}} will auto-complete the quest
</poem>
}}

==Introduction ==
[[File:Monkey Madness I - Ape Atoll map.png|thumb|400px|All the routes you'll need to take in the quest.]]

[[Ape Atoll]] is one of the most dangerous areas in the game. All native creatures other than the level 5 [[Bird|birds]] will be [[aggressive]] towards you, regardless of your [[combat level]], whenever you're not wearing the [[monkey greegree]] (made later in the quest). Players can hug the coasts of Ape Atoll when moving to avoid the poisonous snakes and spiders; some snakes may still be encountered this way, but players can simply wait for them to move.

When going to the island, make sure to bring plenty of [[food]], [[Antipoison|antipoison potions]], and an emergency [[teleport]], in addition to any other required items. Switching off [[Auto Retaliate|auto retaliate]] and utilising [[protection prayers]] will greatly improve your chances of survival.

At any point, you can teleport or flee from [[Ape Atoll]] to restock items without losing your progress. To leave the island by boat, simply talk to [[Lumdo]] in the landing area to go from Ape Atoll back to [[Crash Island]], and then talk to [[Waydar]] on Crash Island to go back to the [[Tree Gnome Stronghold]]. To resume the quest, talk to [[Daero]] on the {{FloorNumber|uk=1}} of the [[Grand Tree]] to go back to the hangar, talk to Waydar in the hangar to go back to Crash Island, and finally, talk to Lumdo on Crash Island to return to Ape Atoll.

== Monkey Business ==
{{Needed|three free inventory spaces|recommended=some high-healing [[food]], a form of [[Poison#Curing poison|poison protection]], a [[lockpick]], and a [[knife]] (to prevent the player from needing to bank by cutting the island's [[Pineapple plant|pineapple]] plants for food)}}

=== Chapter 1 ===
'''"In which our hero/heroine finds himself/herself/themself drawn back into Glough's web of deception and deceit."'''[[File:King Narnode Shareen chathead.png|left|frameless]]
Talk to [[King Narnode Shareen]] in the Tree Gnome Stronghold. He will tell you to investigate [[Glough]]'s old shipyard for any trace of the missing 10th Squad, and will give you the [[gnome royal seal]]. Bring this to [[Karamja]] (the ''"Gandius"'' option on the [[glider]] at the top of the [[Grand Tree]] will bring you nearby). ''If you do not have [[monkey bones]] (or a [[monkey corpse]]), kill a monkey while on Karamja to obtain some.''

'''Note: If you leave the quest to do something else, you will need the gnome royal seal to get into the shipyard'''

After arriving, enter the [[Shipyard (Karamja)|shipyard]] north of the glider landing site and speak with [[G.L.O. Caranock]], a gnome officer for the shipyard. He can be found in the building in the southern area, near the fence. He will act very suspiciously when spoken to, and advise you to return to the king.

Return to King Narnode, and he will give you [[Narnode's orders]] and tell you to speak to [[Daero]], the new head tree guardian. Daero can be found on the {{FloorNumber|uk=1}} of the Grand Tree, near [[Blurberry Bar]]. Go through all the chat options with Daero, and ensure you're ready for surviving Ape Atoll; when you're ready, tell him you're prepared to leave. He will then blindfold you and take you to an [[Underground Military Glider Hangar|underground hangar]]. 

Talk to Daero in the hangar and he will introduce you to [[Waydar]], a gnome pilot. Waydar will task you with solving a "reinitialisation" puzzle, which is similar to the [[puzzle box]]es from [[Treasure Trails]]. If you have done these puzzles before, the sliding puzzle will be easy to complete; if not, see below for the solution, or bring 200,000 [[coins]] and go to [[Glough]], who resides in the same area he did during the [[Grand Tree]] quest. 

==== Waydar's puzzle ====
Begin solving the puzzle by clicking on the panel to the south-east by the southern glider. Players can take some [[spare controls]] from the nearby [[crate (Spare controls)|crate]] to view the solution, or simply consult the image below.

[[File:Monkey Madness I - Spare controls.png|thumb|220px|The solution to the reinitialization puzzle.]]

Solve the sliding puzzle as follows (with the piece numbers counting from the top left corner, moving left to right):

{{Checklist|
* Manoeuvre pieces 1, 2, and 3 into their proper positions in the upper left corner.
* Position piece 4 in the upper right corner.
* Position piece 5 under piece 4 (shuffle pieces 4 and 5 around with some others if they are not in the right order).
* Slide piece 4 to the left and piece 5 up; the first row will now be done.
* Repeat this process with second and then third rows, leaving the completed rows intact.
* With rows 1-3 completed, the puzzle should now be solved in terms of 2-piece high columns.
* Position pieces 16 and 21 next to each other, in that order, on the right-hand side of the bottom row. Slide the remaining tiles clockwise to put them in place.
* Repeat this process with pieces 17 and 22, then with pieces 18 and 23.
* Pieces 19, 20, and 24 should now be easily moved to their correct places.
}}

After completing the puzzle, talk to Daero, and then Waydar. The two of you will depart and then land on the aptly named [[Crash Island]], where the 10th Squad also crashed earlier. Ask the nearby sailor [[Lumdo]] to bring you to [[Ape Atoll]], and he will refuse due to conflicting orders from his sergeant, [[Garkor]]; since Lumdo says he will not take orders from you, talk to Waydar again instead (watch out for poisonous [[Scorpion|scorpions]] just to his left, and '''do not''' click "Travel", as this will bring you back to the Grand Tree). After speaking with Waydar, speak with Lumdo once more, and he will now agree to take you to Ape Atoll.

Do not click the 'X' to close the story tablet that shows up after this conversation or it will cancel the subsequent cut-scene and you'll have to go through the chat option again.

===Chapter 2===
[[File:Monkey Madness I - Jail map.png|thumb|230px|The layout of the jail.]]'''"In which our hero/heroine finds themselves engaging in severe quantities of monkey business."'''

====First Contact====
[[File:Aberab chathead.png|left|frameless]]
Once on the Atoll, start going west, sticking to the southern coastline until you reach a [[mahogany]] tree. From here, run north, avoiding the poisonous [[snake]]s, [[scorpion]]s, and [[Jungle spider (Ape Atoll)|jungle spider]]s. If you have the level to use it, activate [[Protect from Missiles]] as you travel north, as a volley of poisoned arrows will start pelting you as you come closer to the Ape Atoll gate. Once you get close enough to the entrance, you will be knocked out and placed in a prison.

After waking up, you will find yourself in a cell guarded by [[Trefaji]] and [[Aberab]], two gorilla guards. Speak to [[Lumo]], one of the captured 10th Squad members, and then prepare to make your escape.

==== Prison Break ====
{{Map:Monkey Mapness}}Throughout this section and any time you are re-jailed, you will need to stay one square away from the northern cell wall (near the patrolling guards' path) if the guards are in front of it. If you're in their range, they will punch you for 10-20 damage. The guards follow a path, which is as follows: one of the guards will patrol the area twice, walking the length of the prison each time, and then leave to rotate shifts. Use the time that the guard moves '''away from your cell door''' to pick the lock. Once out, as the patrolling guard is walking out to switch with the one on standby, '''walk behind him to reach the exit.''' Be prepared to make several attempts.

After successfully making it out of the building, travel north of the jail and then east to find [[Karam]], an assassin and member of the 10th Squad. Karam can be found in the bushes near the crafting shop ([[File:Crafting shop icon.png]]); he is mostly translucent and he can be difficult to spot, so look carefully. After finding him, speak with him to learn about the gnome mage [[Zooknock]].

Refer to the map to the right for relevant locations during the quest. Be wary of all '''yellow''' dots, as they represent poisonous enemies (scorpions, snakes, and spiders), as well as aggressive monkey archers. When you are in the grass, the [[monkey archer]]s do not fire, but it's still a good idea to activate [[Protect from Missiles]] as a precaution.

Once you're ready, travel south, past the large door, while staying in the tall jungle grass. Past the door, go around the castle counter-clockwise until you're at the southern end, and from here, run south-east, until you are between a white building and the castle. To the east of that building is [[Garkor]], the Sergeant of the 10th Squad. Speak with him, and make sure to get all the way through the dialogue to the point where he tells you to go meet [[Zooknock]] in person.

==== Supply Crates ====
[[File:Monkey dentures detail.png|left|frameless|width=65x65]]Sprint west through the grass with Protect from Missiles on. Continue west (south of the buildings) in the grass until you reach the doorway to your north, marked on the map image above. Walk through the door to enter an enclosed area with a sleeping monkey guard, a banana crate, and a dentures crate.

When inside this house, '''stay on the dark brown ground along the walls''', or you'll risk being sent back to jail. The spiders inside are poisonous, but their poison only hits 1 damage, so it might be helpful to save your anti-poisons for later in the quest. There are bananas in the south-western crates next to the ladder if the player needs a source of food. 

From the entrance, head east and then north, and search the stacked crates towards the middle of the room to acquire [[monkey dentures]]. The sleeping guard is a non-threat so long as you don't talk to him; otherwise, he will call for guards. 

After you have the dentures, search the [[Crate_(Temple_of_Marimbo_Dungeon_entrance)|south-easternmost crate]] and a prompt will appear, asking if you're ready to enter the area below. You will take 32.9% of your current hitpoints as fall damage if you drop down into the cavern when entering, so heal up if necessary before attempting it to avoid dying to the poisonous spiders. Your [[Agility]] level has a chance to negate this damage, so a [[summer pie]] or other Agility boost may help avoid fall damage during this section.

Once inside the cavern, '''travel to the north-west part of the area and search the crates''' to acquire the [[m'amulet mould]]. At this point, if you need to resupply, you can exit the city by going to the south-east corner of [[Marim]], running past the guards and the gravedigger, and climbing up the ladder. After climbing up, jump off the bridge, and go back to the boat to [[Crash Island]]. You can also teleport out.{{clear}}[[File:Monkey Madness I - M'amulet mould location.png|thumb|230px|The location of the M'amulet moulds.]]
====The Light at the End of the Tunnel====
{{Needed|the [[monkey dentures]], the [[m'amulet mould]], and a [[gold bar]]|recommended=1-2 [[antipoison]] potions, 4+ [[energy potion|energy]], or [[Stamina potion|stamina potions]] (or other energy-restoring items such as the [[Explorer's ring 1|explorer's ring]]), 4+ [[prayer potion]]s, some [[food]], a [[plank]] (not necessary if traversing the traps correctly, but can be obtained during the quest anyway), and a [[one-click teleport]]}}

''Note: [[Strange fruit]] are handy for this section as they also cure poison. You will spend about 220% total run energy to traverse to the end of the tunnel at minimum weight, with no energy conserving mechanics; bring enough energy restore to accommodate.'' 



It is '''highly recommended''' to teleport out and bank at this point. This section involves running past dozens of accurate [[Monkey Zombie|monkey zombies]] and [[Skeleton (Ape Atoll)|gorilla skeletons]] which can hit up to 7 and 12 respectively, all while inside a long and tight multicombat tunnel. [[File:ironman chat badge.png]] [[Ironmen]] or other accounts who don't have access to [[energy potion|energy]] or [[prayer potions]] are advised to use other [[Prayer items|prayer-boosting items]], in order to reduce prayer drain. Refer to the table below for the various threats faced throughout the dungeon.[[File:Ape Atoll Dungeon map.png|thumb|380px|[[Ape Atoll Dungeon]]]]
[[File:Ape Atoll Dungeon.png|thumb|width=350x350|Players will need to run under rock structures when navigating the dungeon.]]
When you're ready, travel back to the southern part of Ape Atoll from the Gnome Hangar by speaking to Daero in the Grand Tree. Once you arrive, head west of [[Lumdo]], and enter the [[Ape Atoll Dungeon|tunnel]] ([[File:Dungeon icon.png]]) below.

As soon as you enter, you'll see the screen will start to shake. Rumbling noises will play, and rocks will begin to fall; until you reach the tunnel, you are at '''constant''' risk of taking damage. Use the map to the right to navigate the dungeon.
{| class="wikitable"
|+Dungeon threats and obstacles

!Action

|[[File:Monkey Zombie.png|center|200x200px]]

|[[Protect from Melee]] [[File:Protect from Melee.png|frameless]]
|30
|-
|[[File:Skeleton (Ape Atoll).png|center|200x200px]]

|[[Protect from Melee]] [[File:Protect from Melee.png|frameless]]

|Floor spikes

|Avoid the dot clusters on the ground

|Step on the square '''in front''' of the rock, and then run through by clicking the other side, to avoid all damage. A plank can also be placed on the flat rock to pass the trap.
|2

|You can stand under the overhangs to avoid the falling rocks if you are low on food.
|N/A
|}
Even with consistent running, it will take at least three minutes of running to navigate the dungeon. Be prepared to drink your prayer and energy restoring potions, as well as food, when necessary.

At the end of the tunnel, you will find the gnomes [[Zooknock]], [[Waymottin]] and [[Bunkwicket]]. Talk to Zooknock, and then use your [[m'amulet mould]], [[gold bar]], and [[monkey dentures]] on him and speak to him again. '''Make sure he gives you an [[enchanted bar]] before leaving the tunnel'''. Additionally, '''do not to use the m'amulet mould on Zooknock when you already have the enchanted bar''', as he will keep the mould and you will have to obtain another. Teleport out, and bank if necessary. If you have not gone through all the dialogue with Garkor you will not be able to use the items on him.{{clear}}

====Amulet of Monkeyspeak====
{{Needed|the [[enchanted bar]], the [[m'amulet mould]], a [[ball of wool]], and five free inventory slots for the bananas|recommended=1-2 [[antipoison]] potions, a one-click teleport, and some food.}}

'''''Note''': If you are going to do [[Recipe for Disaster]], bring a ranged or magic weapon to kill a monkey guard and archer to save time later. Low level Ironmen can use rune spear (kp) from Tai Bwo Wannai Trio if the healing is too much to handle.''
[[File:Zombie monkey (item) detail.png|left|frameless|width=85x85]]
After restocking, return to [[Ape Atoll]] and continue north-west until you are placed in the jail cell again. Escape the cell as before, then travel east towards the temple.

You must now descend through the trapdoor to the east in the temple (see map). Activate [[Protect from Melee]] before entering the temple to block attacks from the powerful [[monkey guards]]. 

Most of the guards can simply be run through, but the ones with beards near the trapdoor will act as walls, trapping you if they surround you. The easiest method to get past the bearded gorillas is to go upstairs, and then climb down the ladder on the east side to get close to the trapdoor. If you choose to walk to the ladder directly, make sure to wait until the guards walk away; players can hide in the north-east corner of the temple until there is enough room to descend. If you're surrounded by guards and can't move, teleport out immediately; if you're trapped near the ladder, go up the stairs and hop worlds. Note that the ladder near the trap door can only be climbed up from the EAST side.
[[File:Wall of flame.png|thumb|width=429x429|The wall of flame.]]
Go down the trapdoor while still praying against melee. Ignore the [[monkey zombie]]s (or kill one and pick up the bones if you want to do [[Recipe for Disaster]] later), and swiftly use the [[enchanted bar]] on the nearby [[wall of flame]]s to make an unstrung [[M'speak amulet (unstrung)|monkey amulet]]. Either travel back up the ladder or find a [[safespot]] to get away from the foes, and use the [[ball of wool]] with the unstrung amulet to create the full [[m'speak amulet]]. Be aware that if you ascend the ladder, you will be attacked and can be trapped as before, so ensure Protect from Melee is active.

====A Monkey's Uncle====
[[File:Monkey talisman detail.png|left|frameless|width=85x85]]
Head to the building west of the jail, making sure to sneak through the grass. You'll see a banana garden that has a [[Monkey Child]] in it, which is being patrolled by [[The Monkey's Aunt]]. The aunt follows a predetermined path, which loops around the wooden house by the garden; do not enter the garden when the monkey's aunt is present, or she will call for guards to arrest you. Note that if you're caught, you can sometimes escape without being arrested by quickly climbing up the ladder in the building nearby before the monkeys reach you. Do not speak to [[The Monkey's Uncle]] upstairs, as he will also call the guards. You can use the abandoned building next to the banana garden to quickly access the garden, as it has a connecting corridor out of sight.

Wait for the aunt to walk away, then speak to the monkey child, and tell him that you are his uncle. After he reveals that he is supposed to collect 20 [[bananas]], search and pick five bananas from the nearby trees. The monkey cannot count, so you do not necessarily need 20, as long as you give him at least 5. Give him the bananas, then wait for a few moments in the corridor, and return to the monkey child when the aunt is away. Ask the child to lend you his [[monkey talisman]].

=====Multiple talismans=====
You can obtain more than one talisman without dropping your current ones, so be sure to get an additional talisman for each [[greegree]] you want (four in total are needed for all quests; see below). You will need to speak to the monkey child again for each one, telling him you have lost the talisman, which will cause him to cry. Waiting for the aunt to pass by the child again will make the monkey child have another talisman available; Alternatively, you can switch worlds to potentially make the process go faster. If you talk to the child again before the aunt has passed by, you will get the message "The monkey child is too busy crying..."

Note that after you make your first greegree, talismans can be purchased from [[Tutab's Magical Market]] for 1000 [[coins]]. Tutab will not open his shop unless you are in monkey form.

=====Multiple greegrees=====
For the [[Recipe for Disaster]] quest, you will need an additional three greegrees (four in total). Each of these requires one talisman, for a total of four talismans plus their respective monkey bones if making all talismans in one go. You will save yourself several trips if you obtain all of these materials before going to [[Zooknock]] in the next section. You must kill one of each of the following:
{| class="wikitable"
|+Greegrees
!Type
!Bones/corpse from
!Quest
|-
|{{Ilink|Karamjan monkey greegree|txt=Karamjan monkey|pic=Karamjan monkey greegree}}
|[[File:Monkey.png|frameless|width=138x138]]
|Monkey Madness I
|-
|{{Ilink|Ninja monkey greegree|txt=Ninja monkey|pic=Ninja monkey greegree (medium)}}
|[[File:Medium ninja monkey.png|frameless|width=135x135]]
|[[Freeing King Awowogei]]
|-
|{{Ilink|Gorilla greegree|txt=Gorilla|pic=Gorilla greegree}}

|-

|}
'''Note''': Some monkeys will have large and small variants; both will work for the quests, and merely change the size of your monkey form.

==== Making the greegrees ====
Once you have all of the required bones (or monkey corpse) and talismans, you must run through the tunnels again, bringing your items to Zooknock. Travel through the tunnel for a second time, and when you reach the end, '''use''' the [[monkey talisman]] and all monkey bones (or corpses) on [[Zooknock]].

After a brief delay and conversation, you will get a greegree corresponding to the bones you used on him. To make another greegree, you have to first talk to him again and ask him if he can make another. '''If you cancel the conversation accidentally, ask how to make the monkey talisman and it will resume.''' If for some reason this doesn't work, try the [[drop trick]] with your previous greegrees, and go through the dialogue again. After all desired greegrees are made, bank or stock up as needed, and return to [[Ape Atoll]].

===Chapter 3===
'''<nowiki>''In which our hero/heroine finds himself/herself contending with life as a monkey.''</nowiki>'''

{{Needed|[[Karamjan monkey greegree]], [[m'speak amulet]], 1 free inventory space, and a method of reaching [[Ardougne]]}}
[[File:Awowogei chathead.png|left|frameless]]
In this chapter, you will be required to speak with [[Awowogei|King Awowogei]] and retrieve a monkey from the [[Ardougne Zoo]]. If you need to bank or in order to be more efficient, '''you can skip to retrieving the monkey in Ardougne''' (section "Monkey See, Monkey Zoo" below), and then speak with Awowogei when you return to Ape Atoll (steps 1-3).

1- Wield the [[monkey greegree]] to become a monkey. In this form, you will not be approached or attacked by any of the monkeys or wildlife on the island, and you cannot engage in combat. As a Karamjan monkey, return to [[Garkor]] and speak with him. If you are transformed into a different kind of monkey, he'll tell you to change and come back. After you finish talking, with the m'speak amulet equipped, talk to the [[Elder Guard]] near Garkor. The guard will tell you to speak with [[Kruk]].

2- From the guard, run west until you pass the main gate into [[Marim]] and have reached the Western shore of Ape Atoll (do not exit the gate). Head slightly south and follow the ground path up the western hill, then climb up the bamboo ladder at the top and cross the bamboo bridge. Climb down the ladder on the east side and speak to Kruk. After the conversation ends, he will conveniently take you back to [[King Awowogei]].

3- Speak to Awowogei and offer an alliance, and he will ask you to rescue a monkey from the [[Ardougne Zoo]]. Teleport out and make your way to Ardougne.[[File:Monkey Madness I - G.L.O. and Awowogei.png|thumb|300x300px|Caranock, Waydar and King Awowogei plotting.]]

====Monkey See, Monkey Zoo====
Once in [[Ardougne]], ensure you have an empty inventory space and then head to the south-west corner and enter the zoo. Wield the [[greegree]] inside the zoo area to become a monkey, and then speak to the [[Monkey minder]], who will place you in the cage. Speak with a [[Monkey (Monkey Madness I)|monkey]] (the only one with a "Talk" option), and it'll hop into your inventory; then, unequip the greegree and speak to the Monkey minder again. He will be confused and let you out.
You will now need to travel '''by foot''' to the [[Gnome Stronghold]], before climbing up to the {{FloorNumber|uk=1}} and talking to [[Daero]] to travel back to Ape Atoll as usual. While the monkey is in your inventory, you cannot use any form of teleportation (including [[random events]], spirit trees and fairy rings), as doing so will remove the monkey from your inventory, requiring you to re-obtain it from the zoo.
:''Extra: Your new monkey friend will supply you with some humorous dialogue, and examining him will get you poked. If you have any [[bananas]] in your inventory, he will most likely eat them. There is also a 1/256 chance that he will give you a [[clue scroll (medium)]] if you use bananas on him directly. Note that you must go through the entire dialogue. Only one clue may be obtained per player{{CiteTwitter|author=Mod Ash|url=https://twitter.com/JagexAsh/status/707250010379952128|date=9 March 2016|archiveurl=https://reldo.runescape.wiki/i/101498|archivedate=30 May 2020|quote=1/256, and it's once only per player.}}.''

Return to Ape Atoll, and equip your greegree and amulet to speak with Awowogei again. ''(If you have spoken before, simply speak to the Elder Guard to be let in; if not, refer to the section above).'' The monkey will automatically leave your backpack after speaking to Awowogei, and the king will consider an alliance.

Speak with Garkor to begin Chapter 4.

===Chapter 4, "The Final Battle"===
{{Needed|[[Greegree]], food, an antipoison, and [[combat equipment]] to defeat a level 195 [[Jungle Demon]]}}
[[File:Garkor chathead.png|left|frameless]]
Speak with Garkor one more time (as a human or monkey) to receive a [[10th squad sigil]]. '''''Do not equip it until you are ready for the final battle'''''. Make sure to hold onto the sigil, as if you lose it, Garkor will send you to [[Waymottin]] at the end of [[Zooknock]]'s tunnel to reclaim it.[[File:Monkey Madness I - Jungle Demon fight.png|thumb|350px|The 10th squad and the player fight the demon.]]

Bank in preparation for the [[Jungle Demon]] fight, and ensure you have full [[prayer points]] and health. When you are ready to fight, wear the sigil, and you will be promptly teleported to the Jungle Demon's arena.

'''note:'''
* You can wear the sigil, to start the fight, from anywhere in RuneScape; no need to return to Ape Atoll.
* Although you "wear" the sigil, it stays in your inventory and doesn't unequip your neck-slot equipment.
* Activate Protect from Magic '''''before''''' teleporting, and drink any necessary [[potions]].
* Switch to your weapon/unequip your greegree before teleporting. '''You will lose your greegree''' if you have it equiped and your inventory is full when you teleport.

Upon landing in the arena, you'll be confronted by a level 195 Jungle Demon which can hit up to 32 with both magic and melee. Protection prayers reduce all damage to zero; as such, if the player stands out of melee range, the fight can be completed by simply leaving on Protect from Magic and attacking the demon with Magic or Ranged. Note that the demon's melee attack can hit you even if you're two squares away, similar to a halberd; ensure you're three or more squares away to avoid it. Additionally, '''standing still''' once you are teleported to the arena will keep a distance between you and the Demon, who will not move until you do, although it will still attack you with Magic.

The 10th Squad gnomes will be nearby to assist with the fight, but '''you must deal the final blow to the demon yourself.''' If the gnomes deal the final strike, he will heal around 20 percent of his health (anywhere from 12%-25%); when the demon reaches 1 hitpoint, there will be a period where the gnomes stop attacking, so use this window to deal the final blow yourself.

The demon is weakest to Ranged, although Magic is fairly effective. Players should focus on their combat style with the highest damage. Note that the [[dwarf multicannon]] is effective against the demon, allowing [[pure]] accounts to kill him without gaining any [[Hitpoints]] experience; additionally, despite being from the jungle, he is not immune to poison. Melee is not recommended, but players looking to use melee combat will benefit from using rings of recoil, as the demon's melee attacks hit very accurately.
There is a [[safespot]] in the arena, across a bridge on the outside edge. When you run to the safespot, he will make his way to the bridge, but won't be able to fit over it, allowing you to easily attack him with Magic or Ranged while leaving up Protect from Magic.

If you die during the fight, the sigil can be reclaimed from Death's Office. No need to run through the Ape Atoll Dungeon.

==Finishing up==
[[File:Diamond detail.png|left|frameless|width=57x57]]
Once the Jungle Demon is slain, speak to Garkor, who is indicated by an arrow. He will tell you to speak to Zooknock; speak to Zooknock nearby to get the option to be teleported back to Ape Atoll. If players decline the offer and stay, they are free to explore the [[Banana plantation (Ape Atoll)|banana plantation]], but Zooknock will leave, requiring players to speak to [[Bonzara]] instead if they want to be teleported. He can be found in the crack at the base of the statue.
*'''{{Colour|red|Warning}}:''' The gnomes will teleport you into the jungle on Ape Atoll. It is advised to simply teleport off of the island as quickly as possible; players wishing to stay on the island should immediately equip their greegree after being teleported by the gnomes.
Escape the island however you like, either through the main gates as a monkey, with any form of teleport, or by jumping off the bridge in the south-east part of the city, and go back to the [[Gnome Stronghold]].

Speak with [[King Narnode]] in the [[Grand Tree]] to finish the quest.

'''Congratulations, quest complete! '''

The king will then redirect you to [[Daero]] for your training as a new 10th squad member, so go have a chat with Daero for some optional experience rewards.

==Rewards==
{{Quest rewards
|name = Monkey Madness I
|image = [[File:Monkey Madness I reward scroll.png|centre]]
|qp = 3
|rewards = *10,000 [[Coins]]
*3 [[Diamond]]s
*Speaking to [[Daero]] after the quest gives the choice of either, 35,000 [[experience]] in {{SCP|Attack}} [[Attack]] and {{SCP|Defence}} [[Defence]] and 20,000 [[experience]] in {{SCP|Strength}} [[Strength]] and {{SCP|Hitpoints}} [[Hitpoints]] '''OR''' 35,000 [[experience]] in {{SCP|Strength}} [[Strength]] and {{SCP|Hitpoints}} [[Hitpoints]] and 20,000 [[experience]] in {{SCP|Attack}} [[Attack]] and {{SCP|Defence}} [[Defence]]. The skills you tell Daero you want to focus on will receive 35,000 XP each, and the others 20,000 each.
*The ability to buy and wield the [[Dragon scimitar]].
*Full access to Ape Atoll, with the exception of the bank and javelin store, which are unlocked after [[Monkey Madness II]].
}}

After the quest, players retain the ability to turn into a monkey in Ape Atoll & [[Ardougne Zoo]], using the [[Monkey greegree]]. The [[Jungle Demon]] can also now be fought in the [[Nightmare Zone]].
'''Note:''' while it was previously impossible to return to Ape Atoll after quest completion without receiving experience from Daero, an [[Update:The Garden of Death & More#Poll 77 Changes|update]] in November 2022 now allows the training to be ignored before returning.

== Required for completing ==
Completion of Monkey Madness I is required for the following:
*[[Recipe for Disaster/Freeing King Awowogei]]
*[[Monkey Madness II]]

==Trivia==
*If you talk to [[Zeke]], the scimitar seller of [[Al Kharid]], and ask him for a [[Dragon scimitar]], he will respond by saying, "The banana-brained nitwits who make them would never sell any to me. Seriously, you'll be a monkey's uncle before you hold a Dragon Scimitar," referencing when you pretend to be a monkey's uncle to obtain the [[monkey talisman]].
*If you have started [[Fairytale II - Cure a Queen]], completing this quest and receiving Daero's training unlocks a new [[fairy ring]] location in the [[Ape Atoll Agility Course|Ape Atoll agility course]]. This small area has two exits, one requires a [[ninja greegree]], as well as 48 [[Agility]] to continue through the remainder of the agility course. The other is a much slower route and crosses the nearby gorge by climbing the tower and crossing the rope bridge.
*If you wear a [[M'speak amulet]] and listen to the [[enchanted bar]], it says "I'm stuck in this horrible gold bar!"
*The chapter cards and cutscenes in this quest are references to the adventure game series ''[[wikipedia:Monkey_Island|Monkey Island]]''.
*Upon first talking to [[Awowogei]], [[Uwogo]] says to "never trust a northern monkey". This is about the friendly rivalry between the north and the south within the UK (Northern Monkeys, Southern Fairies).
*You can pick up the Karamjan monkey King Awowogei requests after bringing him to the island. If a player picks up the monkey delivered to King Awowogei after bringing him to the king, travels to Karamja by foot, and drops the monkey, the player will receive their level in Thieving experience multiplied by two.
*[[Karam]] seems to be a reference to Kalam Mekhar (a character from Steven Erikson's [[wikipedia:Malazan_Book_of_the_Fallen|Malazan book of the Fallen]]) and the 10th squad in general could be a reference to the Bridgeburners 9th squad from the same series.
*This entire quest was created by one intern at Jagex during a summer internship.
*If you give a monkey talisman back to the monkey child, he will be grateful and hug you.
